title: Paroxysmal Cough - Pertussis (Whooping Cough)
chief_complaint: 2-month-old infant with 2 weeks of cough that has become paroxysmal with inspiratory whoop, post-tussive emesis, and cyanosis during coughing spells. Afebrile. Mother has chronic cough.

physical_exam.chest: Paroxysmal cough with inspiratory whoop, clear between spells, no wheeze

hidden_data.mother_cough: Mother has chronic cough for 4 weeks

key_learning_points: Infants <4 months at highest risk for apnea and death
Tdap vaccine in pregnancy for prevention

patient_responses.cough: She coughs so hard she turns blue and stops breathing
patient_responses.immunization: She is only 2 months old, hasn't received DTaP yet
patient_responses.mother: I've been coughing for a month, thought it was just a cold

feedback.incorrect_message: This is pertussis. The paroxysmal cough + lymphocytosis + PCR confirm it.
feedback.attending_note: Pertussis management: Azithromycin 10mg/kg/day x5 days. Droplet precautions. Monitor for apnea (continuous pulse oximetry). Supportive care: oxygen, suctioning. Treat close contacts with azithromycin prophylaxis. Tdap in each pregnancy to protect newborns.
